content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# ============================================================================
3	# Hermes Agent Installer
4	# ============================================================================
5	# Installation script for Linux and macOS.
6	# Uses uv for fast Python provisioning and package management.
7	#
8	# Usage:
9	#   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent/main/scripts/install.sh | bash
10	#
11	# Or with options:
12	#   curl -fsSL ... | bash -s -- --no-venv --skip-setup
13	#
14	# ============================================================================
15	
16	set -e
17	
18	# Colors
19	RED='\033[0;31m'
20	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
21	YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
22	BLUE='\033[0;34m'
23	MAGENTA='\033[0;35m'
24	CYAN='\033[0;36m'
25	NC='\033[0m' # No Color
26	BOLD='\033[1m'
27	
28	# Configuration
29	REPO_URL_SSH="git@github.com:NousResearch/hermes-agent.git"
30	REPO_URL_HTTPS="https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent.git"
31	HERMES_HOME="$HOME/.hermes"
32	INSTALL_DIR="${HERMES_INSTALL_DIR:-$HERMES_HOME/hermes-agent}"
33	PYTHON_VERSION="3.11"
34	NODE_VERSION="22"
35	
36	# Options
37	USE_VENV=true
38	RUN_SETUP=true
39	BRANCH="main"
40	
41	# Detect non-interactive mode (e.g. curl | bash)
42	# When stdin is not a terminal, read -p will fail with EOF,
43	# causing set -e to silently abort the entire script.
44	if [ -t 0 ]; then
45	    IS_INTERACTIVE=true
46	else
47	    IS_INTERACTIVE=false
48	fi
49	
50	# Parse arguments
51	while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
52	    case $1 in
53	        --no-venv)
54	            USE_VENV=false
55	            shift
56	            ;;
57	        --skip-setup)
58	            RUN_SETUP=false
59	            shift
60	            ;;
61	        --branch)
62	            BRANCH="$2"
63	            shift 2
64	            ;;
65	        --dir)
66	            INSTALL_DIR="$2"
67	            shift 2
68	            ;;
69	        -h|--help)
70	            echo "Hermes Agent Installer"
71	            echo ""
72	            echo "Usage: install.sh [OPTIONS]"
73	            echo ""
74	            echo "Options:"
75	            echo "  --no-venv      Don't create virtual environment"
76	            echo "  --skip-setup   Skip interactive setup wizard"
77	            echo "  --branch NAME  Git branch to install (default: main)"
78	            echo "  --dir PATH     Installation directory (default: ~/.hermes/hermes-agent)"
79	            echo "  -h, --help     Show this help"
80	            exit 0
81	            ;;
82	        *)
83	            echo "Unknown option: $1"
84	            exit 1
85	            ;;
86	    esac
87	done
88	
89	# ============================================================================
90	# Helper functions
91	# ============================================================================
92	
93	print_banner() {
94	    echo ""
95	    echo -e "${MAGENTA}${BOLD}"
96	    echo "┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐"
97	    echo "│             ⚕ Hermes Agent Installer                    │"
98	    echo "├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤"
99	    echo "│  An open source AI agent by Nous Research.              │"
100	    echo "└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘"

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
